page: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. While single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average tier, the massive core count drives multi-core performance into the top tier. Specifically, the stress-ng multi-core test and Passmark CPU score rank in the top 10% of all tested servers. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are also strong, with Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction tests reaching top-tier status, supported by hardware-level AVX-512 and AES instruction sets.

Memory bandwidth is strong, with cached read speeds of 6701.0 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 337.73 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25%. This high memory throughput, combined with 50 Gbps network bandwidth, supports intensive data operations, as evidenced by strong database performance in Passmark and Redis SET benchmarks. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all disk I/O must traverse the network, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. This server is suited for large-scale multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities but top-tier multi-core execution, with stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and cryptographic tests ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 337.73 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage for disk-heavy operations. This configuration is suited for highly parallelized workloads, database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network speed, optimized for multi-core workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal platform featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for highly parallelized enterprise workloads.

512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cryptographic execution in the top 10% of benchmarks

Suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and software compilation
